factors.name: Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: Strong May NFP report (172K jobs vs 85K expected) has triggered hawkish Fed repricing; bond market now pricing 25bps rate hike in 2026. Chair Warsh faces policy tests with Trump wanting lower rates but data supporting tighter policy.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surge
factors.summary: 10-year and 2-year yields rocketed higher post-NFP (2-year +12bps, 10-year +6bps); elevated yields pressure dividend-heavy Dow components (UNH, utilities, REITs) and increase discount rate on equity valuations.

factors.name: Tech/Chip Sector Selloff
factors.summary: NASDAQ suffered worst day since April 2025 (down 4.18%, -1,121 points on record), with chip stocks (Marvell, Micron) collapsing. Dow's tech/industrial exposure (MSFT, BA) faces spillover pressure. Meta considering tens of billions equity raise signals capital dilution concerns.

factors.name: Broad Equity Selloff Momentum
factors.summary: S&P 500 down 2.65%, wiping $1.8 trillion. Friday's selloff accelerated into close; Dow itself down 1.35% but lagging the broader indices suggests defensive rebalancing into dividend names—temporary support but fragile.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD rallied to ~100.10 post-NFP; strong dollar pressures multinational earnings translation (Dow has ~50% international revenue). Dow heavyweights like CAT, BA, GE face headwinds on FX conversion.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Escalation
factors.summary: US strikes on Iranian radar sites, Gulf flare-up, and Houthi threats to Bab el-Mandeb Strait raise oil/energy volatility. Risk-off sentiment depresses cyclicals; Trump's claim Iran talks 'going well' offers limited credibility given active military engagement.

factors.name: Earnings Season Resilience Question
factors.summary: News headline 'resilient, but for how long?' captures investor hesitation. Strong jobs data now raises wage/margin compression concerns; Dow heavyweights face cost-push pressure ahead of next earnings cycle.

factors.name: Dow-Specific Composition Risk
factors.summary: Price-weighted index means MSFT, UNH, GS, BA tech/financial exposure gets hit hard in risk-off; industrials (CAT, BA) already weakening on strong USD and geopolitical uncertainty. No offsetting strength visible in names like JNJ (beat talc lawsuit but limited upside driver).

factors.name: Credit/Liquidity Stress Signals
factors.summary: No major credit spread blowout yet, but VIX likely elevated post-NFP (not quoted in data). Tech mega-cap equity raises (Google $85B, Meta considering billions) signal capital-raising desperation; not an immediate credit crisis but dilution pressure on valuations.

factors.name: Trump Administration Policy Uncertainty
factors.summary: Tariff uncertainty, wind energy project cancellations, and pardon for insider-trading former congressman create mixed signals. Trump wants lower rates but Warsh/Fed agenda increasingly hawkish; policy conflict unresolved.

screening.narrative: The Dow sold off 1.35% Friday as a stronger-than-expected May jobs report (172K vs 85K) triggered a sharp repricing of Fed rate-hike expectations, pushing Treasury yields sharply higher and killing momentum in tech/growth stocks—the NASDAQ's worst day since April 2025. While the Dow is price-weighted and less exposed to the hardest-hit chip names, its multinational industrial and financial components (MSFT, CAT, GS, BA) are under pressure from dollar strength, elevated borrowing costs, and geopolitical risk (Iran/Gulf escalation). The selloff is in early-to-mid phase: Friday's decline is fresh but conviction needs confirmation Monday. Earnings season rhetoric ('resilient, but for how long?') reflects investor anxiety about margin compression and capital-raising dilution (Google, Meta) ahead.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Chair Warsh public remarks on rate policy (Trump wants cuts; data supports hikes—policy divergence risk)
ISM Manufacturing PMI & Services PMI release (next week) — key for confirming industrial slowdown vs. labor resilience
Durable goods orders, factory orders, industrial production (next week) — Dow-sensitive cyclical signals
Dow heavyweight earnings revisions and guidance (Q2 forward guidance) — margin compression risk on wage/energy inflation
Oil/energy price action (Brent crude sensitivity to Houthi/Iran escalation) — direct impact on CVX, XOM, and transportation costs
Further tech earnings and capital-raise announcements (MSFT, GOOGL capex guidance) — dilution/valuation concerns
China PMI data (if released this week) — demand signal for Caterpillar, Boeing materials/exports
screening.risk_warning: If Fed officials signal dovishness or pivot away from rate-hike pricing mid-week, yields roll over and equities rebound—particularly tech/growth. If Iran talks genuinely de-escalate or Houthi threats recede, geopolitical risk premium deflates. If upcoming earnings prove resilient despite strong labor data (pointing to productivity gains not wage spiral), the Dow could stabilize and retest resistance. A surprise negative economic data release (ISM, jobless claims) would flip the bias to BUY quickly. Dow's dividend-heavy nature means sharp yield spikes can also trigger rebalancing pain but later attract value buyers.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early Phase 2 (Friday's selloff fresh but underway). Do not chase shorts into the close Monday; wait for Dow to test 50,500–50,650 support and establish whether Friday's decline holds or bounces. If support fails cleanly, enter shorts on retest of 50,800–50,900. If support holds and yields stabilize, await ISM/earnings data before committing. Current -1% intraday losses suggest further downside risk but phase 2 chop is likely—patience favors defined-entry over emotional chasing.

factors.name: Strong NFP / Fed Rate Hike Expectations
factors.summary: May NFP came in at 172K vs 85K expected (Bloomberg, MarketWatch), driving hawkish Fed repricing. KPMG Chief Economist noted bond markets now pricing 25bp rate hike in 2026, a significant shift from prior easing expectations.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Surge
factors.summary: 10-year yield surged +6bp, 2-year +12bp following NFP (FXStreet). Higher rates pressure rate-sensitive Dow components (financials initially benefit but equity multiples compress; industrials face higher cost of capital).

factors.name: Tech Sector Collapse
factors.summary: NASDAQ down 4.18% worst day since April 2025, S&P 500 -2.65%. Semiconductor stocks crashed hardest in 6 years (MarketWatch). Although Dow is less tech-heavy, contagion effect and risk-off sentiment weighs on all indices.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Equity Dilution Fears
factors.summary: Meta considering tens of billions in stock offering following Google's $85B equity sale (Financial Times via multiple sources). Signals AI capex strain and potential sector-wide dilution trend affecting MSFT (Dow component).

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk - Iran Conflict
factors.summary: US attacked Iranian radar sites after drone launches (Al Jazeera, Bloomberg). Ongoing Gulf tensions increase supply chain/energy risks for Dow multinationals despite Trump claiming Iran talks 'going well'.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD rallied to near 100.10 from 99.16 low post-NFP (FXStreet). Dow has ~50% international revenue exposure; stronger dollar creates translation headwinds for multinational earnings.

factors.name: Momentum / Retail Investor Fatigue
factors.summary: Bloomberg noted 'Tech Selloff Tests Retail Investor Strength' - questioning durability of retail bid. Bitcoin crashed below $60K to Oct 2024 lows, signaling broader risk-off in momentum trades.

factors.name: Earnings Season Assessment
factors.summary: Stock Market News headline 'Earnings season takeaways: resilient, but for how long?' suggests earnings held up but forward uncertainty rising. No major Dow component earnings in news cycle to provide concrete directional signal.

factors.name: Boeing Production News
factors.summary: Boeing studying boost to 737 MAX production to highest-ever rate, new assembly line starting July 6. Marginally positive for BA but offset by broader market headwinds and already known production ramp expectations.

factors.name: Sector Rotation
factors.summary: Goldman's Flood sees buying opportunity but notes S&P 500 selloff reached -3% intraday. Dow down only -1.35% vs NASDAQ -4.18% suggests relative outperformance, but still in confirmed downtrend with no reversal signals yet.

confirmation.narrative: Dow is in confirmed bearish phase following stronger-than-expected May NFP (172K vs 85K est) which triggered aggressive repricing of Fed policy toward potential 2026 rate hike. Treasury yields spiked (2Y +12bp, 10Y +6bp), pressuring equity valuations across the board. While Dow's -1.35% decline is modest vs NASDAQ's -4.18% collapse, the index faces headwinds from surging dollar (multinational earnings translation), rising rate environment (cost of capital for industrials), geopolitical risk (Iran conflict), and contagion from tech sector equity dilution fears (Meta/Google capital raises). Current -1.02% 24h move indicates Phase 2 - initial reaction underway but not fully extended.
catalysts_ahead: Next Fed meeting decision (June FOMC if scheduled)
China PMI/growth data (affects CAT, BA, materials demand)
CPI/PPI releases (input cost pressures for industrials)
Dow component earnings (particularly MSFT given AI capex scrutiny)
Iran conflict developments (oil price/supply chain implications)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed officials push back on rate hike expectations in next 48h, (2) 10Y yield reverses below 4.3% (if currently ~4.4%+), (3) Iran de-escalation headline catalyst, (4) Dow reclaims 51,200 resistance on strong volume. Goldman noting 'buying opportunity' suggests institutional bid may emerge at lower levels around 50,400 support.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (Phase 2) after NFP catalyst; wait for bounce to 51,000-51,200 resistance zone to enter shorts, or confirm breakdown below 50,400 support for continuation. Avoid chasing current levels.

Dow's dividend payers and rate-sensitive industrials face margin compression.

factors.summary: Google sold $85B equity, Meta considering tens of billions more for AI capex (MarketWatch 2026-06-05T21:15); large secondary issuance supply depresses sentiment. factors.summary: US struck Iranian drones/radar; Iran targeted US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain (BBC/Al Jazeera 2026-06-06T06:16). Red Sea shipping risk (Bab el-Mandeb) threatens oil supply, raising cost-of-goods pressures on industrials. Uncertainty weighs on risk appetite.

factors.summary: Private credit boom slowing sharply on lending/flows pullback (All News 2026-06-06T06:13); Raizen $13B restructuring (Bloomberg 2026-06-05T23:31) signals credit stress. Tighter financial conditions penalize cyclical capex/M&A.

factors.name: Dollar strength post-NFP
factors.summary: USD rallied to ~100.10 post-NFP (FXStreet 2026-06-05T21:22); Dow has ~50% international revenue — stronger dollar translates foreign earnings lower. No hedge in place; earnings revisions downward likely.

factors.name: China growth concerns + regulatory tightening
factors.summary: China tightening oversight of private investment funds (All News 2026-06-06T06:14); Caterpillar, Boeing, materials demand tied to China PMI near stall. SpaceX facing China website ban (All News 2026-06-06T06:08) signals geopolitical decoupling headwind.

factors.name: Austrian sovereign downgrade
factors.summary: Austria lost last top-tier credit rating due to persistent fiscal deficits (Bloomberg 2026-06-05T05:59); signals broader developed-market credit fatigue. European demand for US exports weakens if EU falters.

factors.name: Prior bias alignment (SELL 7/10 from 2026-06-06 02:57)
factors.summary: Haiku and Sonnet both flagged SELL/BEARISH 7/10 as of early 06-06; fresh selloff validates negative bias. Continuation likely into Monday unless late-day reversal Friday invalidates.

screening.narrative: Friday's strong May NFP (172K jobs vs. 85K expected) triggered a sharp repricing of Fed rate hike odds, lifting 10Y Treasury yields +6bps and 2Y yields +12bps. Tech selloff cascaded through equities (Nasdaq down 4.18%, worst day since April 2025; Marvell, Micron, semiconductor complex crushed), dragging Dow -1.35% in a flight-to-safety rotation. Compounding bearish drivers: Big Tech equity capital raises (Google $85B, Meta tens of billions) flood secondary supply; geopolitical flare-up (US-Iran strikes) risks oil shocks; China regulatory tightening and private credit cooling signal demand weakness. Dow's dividend yields look less attractive as real yields rise; multinational earnings face FX headwinds from a stronger dollar. Phase 2 selloff is now underway, with Monday's open likely to test 50,500–50,200 support unless Fed pivot signals emerge.
catalysts_ahead: Monday 2026-06-09: Likely gap-down open if Asia markets extend Friday's tech rout; watch for any weekend geopolitical escalation
Week of 2026-06-09: ISM Manufacturing PMI (early week) — critical for Dow's industrial weighting; miss expectations would worsen selloff
Week of 2026-06-09: ISM Services PMI, factory orders, durable goods — real-economy hard data to validate/invalidate hot jobs report signal
2026-06-16–2026-06-18 (approx): FOMC expectations reset; if Powell signals hawkish hold, equity weakness extends; dovish pivot could trigger short-covering rally
Ongoing: Fed speaker calendar for rate-hike guidance; any 50bps hike talk would trigger fresh selloff
China PMI/economic data (if released mid-week): validate or refute growth slowdown narrative
screening.risk_warning: Risk to this SELL bias: (1) if Fed Chair Warsh or FOMC speakers signal pause/pivot to dovish language, real yields compress and equities bounce hard—watch for any 'we've done enough' commentary; (2) if US-Iran tension de-escalates abruptly or ceasefire agreed, oil dips and risk appetite recovers; (3) if Monday's US market open is stabilizing (limited gap-down, positive reversal), this may mark exhaustion of Phase 2 move, requiring pullback-entry thesis rather than fresh shorts; (4) strong ISM Manufacturing or durable goods could support Dow cyclicals and validate the jobs report as non-inflationary, triggering re-rating higher.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE 2 (underway, ~24h after catalyst). Do not chase shorts here. Instead, identify retest levels: if Dow bounces to 51,000–51,100 on Monday/Tuesday, consider re-short with tight stop above 51,200. Watch for support at 50,500; if it breaks convincingly, next target 50,200–50,000. Alternatively, wait for ISM Manufacturing (early week) to confirm continued economic stress before adding size—premature entry risks whipsaw into Fed-dovish surprise.

factors.name: Strong US Jobs Report (NFP)
factors.summary: Nonfarm payrolls increased 172,000 in May, well above 85,000 consensus. Per KPMG Chief Economist Diane Swonk and bond market pricing, this has triggered hawkish Fed expectations with 25bps rate hike priced for 2026, pushing Treasury yields sharply higher.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Spike
factors.summary: Strong jobs report caused sharp rise in 10-year and long-end yields, putting downward pressure on rate-sensitive dividend payers and financials that populate the Dow. Higher yields reduce equity valuations and increase cost of capital for cyclicals.

factors.name: Tech/Semiconductor Selloff Spillover
factors.summary: Nasdaq fell 4.18% Friday (worst day since April 2025) with chip sector suffering worst day in 6 years. While Dow is more industrial-tilted, broad market breadth deterioration and retail investor weakness (post-SpaceX IPO chatter) suggest risk-off sentiment spreading to blue chips.

factors.name: Dow Point Performance Friday
factors.summary: Dow fell 1.35% Friday while S&P 500 fell 2.65%. Dow underperformed but still captured downside, consistent with broader equity malaise. 24h change of -1.02% shows continued weakness into session open.

factors.name: Iran-US Geopolitical Escalation
factors.summary: US and Iran exchanged military strikes Friday (Iranian missiles toward Kuwait/Bahrain, US strikes on Iranian radar sites). Ongoing Iran war tensions with Strait of Hormuz/Bab el-Mandeb closure risks are inflationary headwinds, supporting higher yields and weighing on cyclicals.

factors.name: Rate Hike Expectations Shift
factors.summary: Prior reads (Haiku SELL7/10, Sonnet BEARISH7/10) were prescient; NFP has confirmed hawkish pivot away from dovish expectations. Bond market now prices ~25bps hike in 2026, moving away from cuts.

factors.name: Multinational Capex Pullback Risk
factors.summary: Alphabet and Meta signaling massive capex needs ($190B+ and tens of billions respectively) via equity raises, suggesting tech giants may pivot to dilution rather than organic funding. This creates uncertainty for Dow industrials that depend on capex cycles (CAT, GE, HON).

factors.name: Dollar Strength (Post-Jobs)
factors.summary: USD rallied on strong NFP and higher yields; NZD/USD fell toward 0.5790, KRW weakened past 1530. Strong dollar headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure (multinational translation drag).

factors.name: Forward Guidance Uncertainty
factors.summary: No recent Boeing, CAT, or other Dow cyclical earnings guidance in past 24h. Boeing+737 Max ramp (July 6 new line) is positive but offset by broader capex uncertainty and rate headwinds.

factors.name: Chinese Demand Softness
factors.summary: China regulators signaling focus on sustainable returns and warning against concept hype; trade talks with NZ ongoing. No hard PMI/GDP data, but tone suggests caution. CAT/DE demand remains uncertain.

screening.narrative: Strong May jobs report (172k vs 85k expected) triggered a hawkish repricing of Fed policy, with Treasury yields spiking and rate-sensitive equities under pressure. Nasdaq led the selloff (-4.18%, worst since April 2025), and Dow followed with -1.35% Friday and -1.02% in 24h, though it underperformed. Escalating US-Iran military exchanges add geopolitical inflation risk, supporting higher real yields and dampening risk appetite. The prior bearish bias (SELL7/10, BEARISH7/10) has been validated by Friday's action, but the move is now in progress (PHASE_2), and immediate support near 50.5k looms. Any retest of that level or stabilization on Monday could offer a better entry for fresh shorts; chasing the move here risks catching a reversal into jobless claims / ISM data next week.
catalysts_ahead: Jobless Claims (weekly, likely Thursday 2026-06-12)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (likely June 2 or 3 week; prior reads critical for Dow cyclicals)
ISM Services PMI (similar timing)
CPI / PCE inflation data (next scheduled ~mid-June; critical for Fed pivot confirmation)
FOMC Minutes or Powell speak (will clarify hawkish intent)
Dow component earnings updates (JPM, GS, UNH, BA guidance critical)
China PMI / official manufacturing data (demand signal for CAT, DE, materials)
Earnings season for financials (GS, JPM may guide on margin pressure from higher yields)
screening.risk_warning: **Invalidation triggers:** (1) Jobless claims spike to >300k, triggering dovish pivot and yield collapse (would reverse bias BULLISH). (2) Powell or Fed speaker signals no rate hikes yet, or data soften unexpectedly (CPI/PCE cool). (3) US-Iran ceasefire or de-escalation announcement, collapsing geopolitical premium and oil (would reduce inflation narrative). (4) Major Dow component (JPM, GS, CAT) posts strong guidance; earnings surprise to upside. (5) Move extended >2% intraday; Friday's -1.35% may be the initial shock; a Monday rebound or stabilization would invalidate SELL thesis and demand wait-and-see.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is PHASE_2 (underway post-catalyst). Avoid chasing shorts here; instead, wait for intraday retest of resistance 51,200 or a stabilization on Monday morning before adding. If support at 50.5k breaks decisively <50.3k on high volume + jobless claims confirm weakness, then consider continuation shorts. Otherwise, reduce confidence on extended moves and wait for pullback into 51,000–51,100 range for better risk/reward.

factors.summary: 10-year yields spiked Friday following NFP, crushing risk assets. Nasdaq posted worst day since April 2025 (-4.18%); S&P 500 down -2.65%. Rate-sensitive Dow components (utilities, REITs, dividend stocks) face headwinds from higher long-end yields.

factors.name: Tech Sector Selloff & Valuation Reset

factors.summary: No fresh earnings catalyst in news window; upcoming reports will be key (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, BA in focus). Forward guidance from CAT, DE, MMM, HON likely hawkish on margin pressure from higher rates + tariff headwinds.

screening.narrative: US30 extended losses into early June on the back of a blowout May jobs report (172k vs 85k expected), which triggered a sharp repricing of Fed rate hike expectations—bond markets now pricing 25bp hike in 2026. Treasury yields spiked Friday, crushing risk assets (Nasdaq -4.18%, S&P 500 -2.65%) and fueling a broad selloff in equities, crypto, and high-yield credit. The Dow, despite being less tech-heavy, remains exposed to dividend-yield compression from rising long-end yields and multinational earnings translation headwinds from a surging USD. Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran military exchanges, Houthi threats) adds uncertainty, while tariff rhetoric and margin-pressure narratives keep cyclical leaders defensive. The move is already ~1% down in 24h; price action is now in Phase 2 (pullback/retest zone), not fresh.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Services PMI (scheduled mid-week) — Dow has significant services exposure; weak print could confirm slowdown, bullish for recession positioning
Durable Goods Orders & Factory Orders (early-to-mid week) — direct read on CAT, MMM, BA, HON demand; hawkish outlook from guidance could accelerate selloff
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, BA, V) — forward guidance on rates, tariff impact, and margin outlook critical; potential to surprise lower given higher input costs
FOMC meeting (late June) — market will likely chop into this; any hint of accelerated rate path would extend bearish bias
China PMI / hard data releases — Caterpillar, Boeing demand signals critical; slowdown would hurt machinery & aerospace orders
Oil/Energy data (EIA inventory, OPEC updates) — geopolitical risk premium, Iran/Houthi threats; higher oil pushes inflation risk, steepens yield curve
screening.risk_warning: Risk to SELL thesis: (1) If new economic data (ISM, durable goods) surprises to the upside and inflation cools, Fed could pivot dovish, triggering a sharp relief rally in Dow + dividend names. (2) Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran war) could spike oil >$100/bbl, forcing energy sector rebalance and lifting energy + materials stocks (XLE, CVX heavy in Dow). (3) Strong earnings beats from Dow components (especially financials JPM, GS, or healthcare UNH) could restart the bid despite higher yields. (4) A coordinated dovish pivot from global central banks (ECB, RBNZ, BoE) could reduce real yields, supporting equities. Monitor Treasury 10-year for break above 4.5% (would extend downside); break below 4.2% would invalidate bearish bias.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already ~1% in 24h (Phase 2); do not chase short here. Wait for retest of 51,000-51,200 resistance zone OR break of 50,400 support before fresh entry. Best entry on oversold bounce + rejection of higher Treasuries, or on confirmation of ISM/durable goods weakness early next week. Risk/reward deteriorates as yields stabilize.

screening.narrative: The Dow broke a two-month rally on Friday following a 'blowout' jobs report that reignited Fed rate-hike expectations. While Dow fundamentals remain solid (strong earnings season), tariff/trade escalation under Trump, the ongoing Iran conflict, and surging long-end yields are repricing dividend payers and cyclicals lower. Semiconductor sector rolled over (Micron -13%), VIX mean-reversion has kicked in, and big-tech selloff created negative sentiment spillover. Your three consecutive SELL6/10 calls align with emerging weakness, but the move is only ~1% so far—Phase 2, not yet extended.

Apple WWDC keynote (Monday 6/10) — AI strategy make-or-break, could affect Tech/Dow sentiment

screening.risk_warning: Invalidation triggers: (1) Major Fed dovish pivot or rate-cut signal narrows yield curve and rebounds dividend/cyclical names; (2) Ceasefire/resolution in Iran war cuts oil/freight costs, easing input pressures on industrials; (3) Tariff negotiations stall/rollback reduces trade anxiety; (4) Dow component beats on earnings despite guidance warnings suggest recession fears overblown; (5) China stimulus/PMI surprise accelerates demand — CAT, BA, materials spike. Geopolitical de-escalation or macro surprise dovishness could flip bias to BUY quickly.
screening.entry_guidance: Move underway (Phase 2, -1% in 24h); avoid chasing shorts at extremes. Look for retest of 51,000–51,100 resistance on any intraday bounce; if hold below 50,900, consider short bias into 50,500 support. Alternatively, wait for VIX spike >20 or yield pullback (10Y <4.3%) as lower-risk entry for longs (hedge against extension risk).

factors.name: Jobs Report & Rate Hike Bets
factors.summary: Strong US jobs report prompted market bets that Fed will raise rates in 2026 (Bloomberg 09:00 06-06). Rate hikes typically compress equity valuations, especially Dow dividend payers and industrials sensitive to borrowing costs.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Rising
factors.summary: Blowout jobs data drove 10-year yields higher and bonds/stocks lower (investingLive 21:55 06-05). Rising long-end yields harm Dow's dividend-heavy, rate-sensitive names (utilities, financials).

factors.name: Big Tech Shake & Nasdaq Correction
factors.summary: Nasdaq posted biggest daily fall since early 2025 (BBC 21:38 06-05); chip-stock rally reversal noted (Bloomberg 12:58). Dow weights MSFT, AAPL, and chip suppliers; rotation out of mega-cap growth pressures index.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium (Iran)
factors.summary: Iran-US military exchanges at 100-day mark; ballistic missiles intercepted in Gulf (Bloomberg 15:02 06-06). War duration and energy supply uncertainty drive oil volatility and inflation expectations; no resolution in sight.

factors.name: Shipping Costs & Supply Chain Inflation
factors.summary: Asia-to-US container rates spiked 109% since Iran war started (Bloomberg 12:06). Higher freight and fuel costs feed input inflation for Dow industrials (CAT, BA, transportation); margin pressure expected.

factors.name: Oil Inventory Risk & Fuel Shock
factors.summary: US crude inventories at dangerously low levels amid Iran war (MarketWatch 12:00). Airlines and logistics report fuel shocks (All News 13:43, 14:12). Energy cost inflation threatens airline, trucking, and industrial profitability.

factors.name: Tariff Uncertainty & Trade Policy
factors.summary: Trump administration proposing new tariffs on 60 nations citing forced labor (Bloomberg 13:13). Dow has ~50% international revenue; tariffs increase input costs and retaliation risk, especially for industrials (BA, CAT, GE).

factors.name: VIX & Volatility Reversal
factors.summary: Wall Street's 'fear gauge' VIX finally caught up as chip rally reversed (Bloomberg 12:58). Volatility expansion typically pressures equities; risk-off tone persisting into weekend.

factors.name: Deutsche Bank Macro Warning
factors.summary: Deutsche Bank maps volatile '1999 meets 1990' macro outlook (All News 13:00), implying stagflation or structural market stress; analyst-earnings bullishness diverges sharply from macro forecast.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (Mixed Signal)
factors.summary: Rising yields and rate-hike bets typically strengthen USD; Ken Rogoff warns dollar's dominance faces mounting pressures (Bloomberg 12:03). Stronger dollar headwind for Dow multinationals but reflects risk-off.

factors.name: Historical Earnings Strength
factors.summary: Strongest earnings season in years is closing; individual stock analysts rarely this bullish (Bloomberg 13:00). But analyst-macro divergence is extreme; earnings may not sustain into Q3.

factors.name: Fed Chair Warsh Transition Uncertainty
factors.summary: New Fed chair may make swift communication changes; market awaits clarity on policy path (MarketWatch 11:00). Uncertainty ahead of FOMC meetings caps upside.

screening.narrative: US30 is under pressure from a potent confluence: blowout jobs report triggering rate-hike expectations, soaring Treasury yields, and a Nasdaq-led tech selloff that has cascaded into Dow heavyweights (MSFT, AAPL). Geopolitical risk from the Iran conflict (100 days ongoing, no resolution) has spiked shipping costs 109%, feeding input inflation fears for industrials (CAT, BA) already facing tariff headwinds and margin compression. While consensus earnings remain strong, the macro divergence is stark: Deutsche Bank warns of a '1999-meets-1990' volatile outlook, and VIX finally reversed upward. The index is down 1.02% in 24h and -0.58% in 4h—a move already in progress, not nascent. Your prior three reads were SELL 6/10 → SELL 5/10, suggesting weakening conviction, which aligns with the current messy, risk-off but unconfirmed backdrop.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting (early June window) — rate guidance and chair Warsh's communication tone
ISM Manufacturing & Services PMI — direct health check on Dow's industrial base and labor market
Durable goods orders, factory orders — cyclical health of CAT, BA, GE, MMM
Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, HD expected mid-June) — confirm margin resilience or deterioration
Oil prices & energy data — Iran conflict duration and crude supply risk
China PMI and growth data — demand signal for CAT, BA, materials
US-China trade negotiations and tariff announcements — direct margin impact for multinationals
screening.risk_warning: If jobs data surprises cool (lower wage growth, hours cut) and Fed signals pause or pivot to cuts, rate-hike bets will unwind, yields will fall, and equities (especially dividend payers) could rally sharply, invalidating this SELL bias. Similarly, if Iran conflict de-escalates abruptly or geopolitical tensions ease, energy costs and supply-chain inflation fears evaporate, reversing the bearish narrative. Earnings season holdouts from Dow heavyweights could also stabilize sentiment if guidance is resilient.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in progress (down 1% in 24h, Phase 2). Do not chase shorts here; wait for a relief rally toward 50,950–51,050 (mid-range retest) or intraday strength at open, then reassess technicals and news flow before fresh shorts. If index breaks decisively below 50,500, confirm 50,200–50,300 as next support.

factors.summary: Down 0.58% in 4h, -1.02% in 24h. Move already underway; Friday saw 'avalanche of selling' that crushed stocks from record highs earlier in week per MarketWatch.

factors.name: VIX reversion and volatility catch-up
factors.summary: Wall Street's 'fear gauge' finally punched back after semiconductor rally reversed; credit spreads widening signal risk-off sentiment taking hold.

factors.name: Iran war inflation tail-risk

factors.summary: Trump administration discussing new tariffs on 60 nations for forced labor; ongoing Gaza/Lebanon/Ukraine tensions. Dow has ~50% international revenue—tariffs and tariff-war escalation are direct headwinds.

factors.name: June swoon narrative

factors.name: Earnings backdrop

screening.narrative: The Dow rallied into record highs early in the week but reversed sharply Friday on an 'avalanche of selling' triggered by a blowout jobs report, reigniting Fed rate-hike expectations and sending the chip sector into its worst day in 6 years. VIX and volatility metrics are now catching up to repriced risk. The move is in Phase 3—extended downside—with multiple headwinds (higher rate expectations, Iran war inflation tail-risk, tariff uncertainty, AI spending disappointment) combining to pressure the price-weighted index. Dow components like MSFT, CAT, and BA face multinational translation drag and cyclical slowdown risk.

screening.narrative: The Dow has turned negative on a blowout jobs report that raised Fed rate-hike expectations, triggering a classic 'strong data = near-term pain' selloff in rate-sensitive dividend and cyclical names. Simultaneously, semiconductor weakness (worst 6-year day), Iran war escalation, and AI profitability concerns have broken the post-earnings rally momentum. While individual Dow components (UNH, MSFT, JPM, BA) have solid fundamentals, the macro backdrop—higher rates, geopolitical friction, input cost inflation, and potential June seasonal weakness—is pressuring the index. Prior bias was SELL5/10 with no confirmation; today's modest decline (-1%) suggests the move is in early Phase 2, not yet extended or capitulated.

screening.narrative: Dow has pulled back -0.58% in 4h and -1.02% in 24h as a coordinated sell-off crushes equities following a whirlwind week of record highs. The underlying catalyst cocktail is toxic: US-Iran military escalation (100 days in, no ceasefire), fuel/shipping cost shock rippling through airlines and industrials, VIX finally reversing the 'crash-up' in chip/AI stocks, and mounting credit-risk concerns from major asset managers. Prior bias reads (three consecutive SELL5/10 on 2026-06-06) have not triggered confirmation, but the market has now validated the downside thesis with a Friday avalanche.
